Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-0$111
t
Dear Ms. Gordon:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the County of Yuba for
advice regarding campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act').1
QUESTTON
May candidates and committees involved in an election to be held on August 30,
2005, combine the semi-annual statement with the firstpreelection statement due on July
21,2005?
CONCLUSION
In connection with an August 30, 2005 election, candidates and committees may
use a filing schedule that combines the semi-annual campaign staternent with the first
pre-election statement due on Jlu/ry 21,2005.
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
t
€F


I
t
File No. A-05-111
Page No. 2
ANALYSIS
During an election, candidates and committees involved in the election are
required to file two campaign statements disclosing confibutions received and
expenditures made in connection with that election. These pre-election campaign
statements are generally filed 40 days before the electio, and again 12 days Lefore the
election. (Section 84200.8.) Serni-annual statements are filed January3l and July 31,
respectively. (Section 84200.)
There is a mecharrism in the Act that permits the combining of statements when
an overlapping reporting period occurs, or the filing of the semi-annual staternent would
be an additional, unnecessary filing to the two pre-election statements. (Section 84205.)
In this case, filing the two pre-election statements in connection with an August 3CI,2005,
election will satisfu the semi-annual filing requirement.
A campaign statement filing schedulewhich outlines the duedates for filing
statem€nts in connection with the election is enclosed for your conveniemce.
If you have any other questions regmding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5664.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
